senses.definition: the state or quality of being a ruffian; boorish or violent behavior characteristic of a ruffian
examples: The crowd's ruffianage turned a peaceful protest into a chaotic scene.
They condemned the ruffianage that followed the accident.

senses.definition: a state or place associated with ruffians; a community or area dominated by criminals or rough behavior
examples: The town descended into a ruﬁiandom after dusk.
He fled the back alleys, a ruinous ruﬃandom behind him.

senses.definition: to treat someone violently or rudely as a ruffian; to behave like a ruffian
examples: The guard ruffianed the suspect, which sparked an investigation.
He was ruffianed out of the club for assault.

senses.definition: the state or condition of being a ruffian; the characteristics or life of a ruffian
examples: Stories depicted the rough ruffianhood of street life.
He rejected the ruffianhood that his peers celebrated.

senses.definition: the act of behaving like a ruffian; violent or rough conduct
examples: The night was filled with ruffianing and shouting.
Witnesses described the ruffianing that followed the game.

senses.definition: like or characteristic of a ruffian; rough or boorish
examples: His ruffianish manners turned many people away.
They wore a ruffianish look after the brawl.

senses.definition: the ideology or behavior of ruffians; the practice of being a ruffian
examples: The movement was criticized for its thin veiling of ruffianism.
Civic leaders condemned ruffianism in the city.

senses.definition: plural of ruffianism; multiple instances or examples of ruffian behavior or ideology
examples: The reports highlighted various ruffianisms observed downtown.
Academics debated the social roots of ruffianisms.

senses.definition: to make someone or something behave like a ruffian; to roughen or corrupt
examples: The disorder ruffianizes the street corners.
Alcohol can ruffianize crowds quickly.

senses.definition: resembling or characteristic of a ruffian
examples: The alley had a rough, ruffianlike atmosphere.
His ruffianlike glare warned us away.

senses.definition: a male Italian name; historically used to refer to a gentleman in certain contexts; also used in English to refer to a manipulative or fashionable man in some phrases
examples: The artist was friends with the ruﬃano who hosted salons.
In some novels, a ruﬃano moves between courts and cafés.

senses.definition: rough, violent men; criminals or bullies
examples: The guards chased away the ruffians from the square.
Young ruffians caused trouble near the market.

senses.definition: an obsolete or dialect form referring to a rough person; variant spelling of ruffian
examples: Old texts sometimes spell ruffin as ruffin.
The term ruffin appeared in 18th-century literature.

senses.definition: the act of smoothing or ruffing; alternatively the act of making a fabric frill (less common) or a period of weariness in belts; mainly used as a variant of ruffle in verbs
examples: Her dress had delicate ruffing at the sleeves.
The ruffing in the fabric gave it a decorative edge.

senses.definition: plural of ruﬃn; uncommon form referring to rough people or dialectal usage
examples: Old ballads mention the ruﬃns in the alleys.
They spoke of several ruﬃns as troublemakers.

senses.definition: a line or strip of fabric that stands away from the surface; a gathered frill on clothing
examples: The dress has a delicate lace ruffle along the hem.
She brushed the ruffle of her sleeve.

senses.definition: to disturb or annoy someone; to cause to become irritated or upset
examples: The loud music ruffled her concentration.
News of the delay ruffled the passengers.

senses.definition: to make or become creased or wrinkled, especially by touching or handling
examples: She ruffled the papers and spilled ink.
The wind ruffled the leaves.

senses.definition: slightly upset or disturbed; having a wrinkled or wrinkled surface
examples: He looked ruffled after the argument.
The skirt was ruffled at the hem.

senses.definition: without frills or ruffles; plain or simple in appearance
examples: She preferred a ruffleless blouse for the interview.
The tablecloth was plain, ruffleless fabric.

senses.definition: the act of creating a ruffle; a frill or gathered edge
examples: The dress had a soft rufflement at the neckline.
Add a subtle rufflement to the sleeve.

senses.definition: a person who creates frills or ruffles; a device or tool that ruffles fabric
examples: The ruffler attachment helps sew ruffles automatically.
She hired a ruffler to finish the dress.

senses.definition: plural of ruffler; people or devices that create ruffles
examples: Several rufflers were used in the sewing workshop.
Old machines and modern rufflers both served the same purpose.

senses.definition: a type of gathered fabric used as decoration on clothing or accessories
examples: She wore a dress with white lace ruffles.
The shirt had ruffles along the sleeve.

senses.definition: to disturb or upset someone; to cause slight flutter or anger
examples: The news ruffles the calm morning.
Her ruffles his orderly mind.

senses.definition: more ruffian-like; more rough or boorish
examples: The crowd grew rufflier as dusk fell.
That attitude makes him rufflier than before.

senses.definition: most ruffian-like; the peak of roughness or ferocity
examples: They faced the ruffliest mob in town.
That was the ruffliest moment of the night.
